decision: Shell redesign: universal topbar owns user actions + project switcher; project-scoped sidebar only inside /projects/[slug]/*
reasoning: Sidebar on /projects was redundant with the page's own project list; user actions were inconsistent across pages
expected_outcome: Consistent global actions, non-confusing navigation, sidebar only where it adds value

decision: api_v2 uses global ClerkAuthGuard (APP_GUARD) with @Public() opt-out; verifyToken standalone from @clerk/backend
reasoning: NestJS APP_GUARD is the secure-by-default pattern; @Public() is explicit; verifyToken avoids needing a full Request adapter
expected_outcome: All routes protected by default; easy to add new public endpoints; Clerk JWT verified on every request

decision: @clerk/nextjs pinned to ^7 (not ^6); proxy.ts used instead of middleware.ts
reasoning: v6 has Turbopack incompatibility with Next.js 16; Next.js 16 deprecated middleware.ts in favour of proxy.ts
expected_outcome: No Turbopack build errors; auth middleware runs correctly in Next.js 16

decision: Clerk <SignIn>/<SignUp> components with appearance customisation rather than fully custom auth UI
reasoning: Custom auth UI is high effort with low ROI at this stage; Clerk components with CSS var styling match the design system adequately
expected_outcome: Functional auth UI that matches theme; can be replaced with custom UI later if needed

decision: Codesearch now uses intent-aware reranking plus query variants, validated by a depth-tier benchmark script
reasoning: Adversarial prompts were ranking README/spec files above implementation files; benchmark-first tuning made regressions measurable and fixable
expected_outcome: Higher precision on deep/complex retrieval queries with repeatable benchmark evidence for future tuning
